other_observers: Anne Smith; I am the original finder No independent observations
description: Similar to familiar house finches, except for bold head pattern, no eye ring, deeply notched tail. Head pattern was distinctive enough that I knew I had not seen one like this in Boulder. The closest match in my Sibley's guide is with eastern purple finch (F). Photos available.Behaviors: At my backyard feeder (sunflower seeds) for about 15 minutes on Christmas day (2011), with a mixed flock of house finches and American finches. Behaved pretty much the same as the house finches, but readily distinguishable from these by head pattern. My backyard borders on Boulder open space. After feeding for several minutes, the bird flew west, towards foothills. Not seen since.Call: not heardPlumage: n/a
photographer: Photographer: Rolando Garcia, Rolando Garcia, Rolando Garcia
